summary: ts-eslint-helper 4.0.5 executes base64-decoded payload on load
details: index.js line 9 contains a Buffer.from(<...>, "base64").toString("utf8") construct used to reconstruct a hidden payload from an embedded base64 blob at module load time. The package name presents itself as a TypeScript/ESLint helper, but a decode-and-execute primitive in the main entry file is inconsistent with an ESLint helper's declared purpose and matches the shape of obfuscated loader stages used to hide network beacons, credential collection, or downloaded second-stage code from casual inspection. The obfuscation itself, combined with the package's small file count (3 files) and the executable position of the decode in the main module, indicates the package is not a benign helper library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
